artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found KU-controlled public guidance on employee AI use, supported researcher tools, PhD thesis AI disclosure, and scholarly-publication authorship. A COBL news item about an exam declaration template was rejected for claim support because the operative template/guidance is on KUnet. Two KUnet guidance pages were recorded as login-gated after 403 responses and were not bypassed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guideline on the use of AI-assisted technologies for PhD theses
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: if you use AI-assisted technologies in your thesis it must be clearly disclosed, and the use must be described. The AI-assisted technology cannot be listed as an author. You are therefore solely responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University of Copenhagen guidelines on authorship of scholarly publications
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If AI-assisted technologies such as large language models, chatbots or image creating programs have been used in the scholarly work, this must be clearly stated and declared in the publication. Chatbots and the like cannot be listed as co-authors of scholarly work; researchers who use AI-assisted technologies in their work must be able to account for the results.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow employee-guidance claim directly supported by the public shared KUnet source text.
Approved because the evidence directly states employees must avoid ChatGPT or other non-licensed generative AI for UCPH work.
Approved as researcher-tool guidance, not as a broader university-wide procurement conclusion.
Approved as PhD-thesis guidance only; it is not generalized to all student assignments.
Approved as scholarly-publication authorship guidance only, directly tied to the PDF section on AI technologies.

artifacts.title: University of Copenhagen staged AI policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw crawl for University of Copenhagen found official KU-controlled evidence for employee Copilot Enterprise guidance, supported researcher AI tools, PhD thesis disclosure rules, and scholarly-publication authorship treatment of AI-assisted technologies. KUnet teaching and employee guide pages requiring login were recorded but not bypassed, and a COBL news item about an exam declaration template was not used for binding exam-policy claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
KUnet pages that likely contain additional teaching and employee implementation guidance returned HTTP 403 and were not accessed.
The COBL exam declaration news item was treated as discovery context only because the operative declaration template and guidance are on KUnet.
Claims are scoped to employee work, researcher tools, PhD theses, and scholarly publications; no university-wide student-assessment rule is inferred from these sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
